title: valeriana capitata subsp. acutiloba
intro: Valeriana capitata subsp. acutiloba, also known as the Chilean valerian or narrow-leaved valerian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Andes mountains in South America, known for its medicinal properties, which include sedative, anti-anxiety, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as promoting relaxation, reducing stress and insomnia, and alleviating menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Valeriana capitata subsp. acutiloba can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to calm the nervous system and improve sleep quality.
